source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A particle of mass m is released at $t=0$ in the one-dimensional double square well shown in the figure in such a way that its wave function at $t=0$ is just one sinusoidal loop (half a sine wave with nodes just at the edges of the left half of the potential as shown). Find the average value of the energy at $t=0$ (in terms of symbols defined above).

metadata.reasoning_chain: The normalized wave function at $t=0$ is $\psi(x,0)=\sqrt{\frac{2}{a}}\sin\frac{\pi x}{a}$. Thus $$\langle\hat{H}\rangle_{t=0} = -V_{0} -\frac{\hbar^{2}}{2m}\frac{2}{a}\int_{0}^{a}\sin\left(\frac{\pi x}{a}\right)\frac{d^{2}}{dx^{2}}\sin\left(\frac{\pi x}{a}\right)dx = \frac{\hbar^{2}\pi^{2}}{2ma}-V_{0}.$$
